feed.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage

entry.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
entry.summary: The discussion revolves around the technical handling of 'segdata' within a specific infrastructure, highlighting that neither Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) nor storage systems require 'segdata', and consequently, its validation is unnecessary. This point addresses an initial query regarding the necessity and processing of 'segdata'. The dialogue then shifts towards the broader implications of whether the data structure in question is integral to consensus rules necessitating processing by all full nodes or if it stands outside these requirements, thus allowing for current processing without the need for a soft fork. This dichotomy underscores the critical examination of system architecture and the operational norms that could potentially influence future technical decisions or adjustments within the framework.
